title: Dempsey Construction — Post-Meeting Summary (March 18, 2026)
markdown:
# Dempsey Construction — Post-Meeting Summary
**Date:** March 18, 2026 | **Duration:** 28 minutes
**Recording:** [Fathom Link](https://fathom.video/share/_cJvLutVnqZpJwz8RSyBWDvhm3hpy449)

---

## Attendees
| Name | Role | Notes |
|------|------|-------|
| **Justin Headley** | Sagemind AI | Led the call |
| **Drew Wall** | PM team + technology initiatives | Key decision-influencer, focused on system integration |
| **Merissa Fruin** | Sr. Project Coordinator | Saw GCI demo from Tom, very engaged |
| **Bryce Raleigh** | Leadership / Operations | Joined mid-call. Focused on security, trust, and long-term direction |

---

## Key Takeaways

### 1. They have a real pain point — data fragmentation
- Multiple systems: **Procore** (project management), **Monday.com** (task mgmt/CRM/reporting), plus an **on-premise physical server** for file storage
- Massive double-entry problem — same project data updated independently across systems, often outdated or inconsistent
- Drew described departments building independent Monday boards without cross-department coordination

### 2. They already explored Trunk Tools — liked it, couldn't afford it
- Found Trunk Tools "super helpful" but "cost prohibitive"
- They specifically liked the **agent** functionality vs. simple bot/retrieval
- This validates the positioning: **80% of Trunk Tools capability at a fraction of the cost**

### 3. Tom's referral carries strong trust
- Bryce explicitly said: *"Tommy speaks very highly of you"* and *"We trust you because Tommy trusts you"*
- Merissa saw the GCI Data Scout demo on a call with Tom — she was impressed
- The trust is real but fragile — they're in a **"foreign space"** and very aware of what they don't know

### 4. Security is a top concern
- Bryce raised it directly: *"Our founder John will want to make sure there's no chance someone could get in and cause a problem"*
- The on-premise server is a sensitivity point — they may be intentionally air-gapped
- Justin handled this well: baby steps, full transparency, IT team involvement

### 5. ChatGPT is the right platform for them
- Already using ChatGPT in groups (Drew, Bryce, Merissa have business accounts)
- Others in the company on free versions — needs cleanup but familiarity is there
- Justin correctly positioned ChatGPT for their use case, Claude for dev work

### 6. They want to move toward agentic capabilities
- Bryce articulated the bot vs. agent distinction clearly: *"retrieval vs. action"*
- Specifically interested in AI that can **check submittals, create RFIs** — like an AI project engineer
- Bryce: *"If you can create an AI project engineer, you will be our new best friend"*
- This is a future upsell, not Phase 1 — but important to keep in the roadmap

### 7. No cloud file storage — physical server only
- They do NOT have a cloud-based file server (no Egnyte, no SharePoint, no Google Drive)
- This means the **Procore integration is even more critical** as the primary data source for the AI
- Server integration is a separate, more complex conversation requiring IT involvement

---

## What Went Well
- **Form responses saved time** — Justin acknowledged this and it set a professional tone
- **GCI demo credibility** — Merissa seeing the Data Scout demo gave instant proof of concept
- **Honest positioning** — Justin was transparent about this being his second engagement, which built trust rather than undermining it
- **Security sensitivity** — Justin's "baby steps" framing on server access was exactly right
- **Platform recommendation** — Correctly steered them to ChatGPT (familiar) rather than pushing Claude

## What Could Improve
- **Pricing wasn't discussed in detail** — They asked about next steps and contracts, but no number was floated. This is fine for a first call, but need to come back with a clear proposal
- **Monday.com integration wasn't explored** — Drew's pain is really about Monday + Procore + CRM fragmentation. The ChatGPT-Procore integration is a start but their bigger pain might be Monday.com data silos
- **ChatGPT account consolidation** — Need to address the fragmented ChatGPT usage (some business, some free) before implementation

---

## Action Items

### Justin (Sagemind)
- [ ] **Draft a proposal/contract** — Tailored to Dempsey, not a copy of GCI's. Should cover:
  - Scope: ChatGPT custom GPT with Procore integration (Phase 1)
  - Pricing: Fixed-price package (recommend K based on prior research)
  - Payment structure: 50/50 or milestone-based
  - Security commitments and data handling
  - What access is needed (Procore admin, ChatGPT account)
- [ ] **Send follow-up email** — Summarize the call, proposed path, and what you need from them
- [ ] **Research Monday.com API** — Understand if/how it could be integrated as a Phase 2 play
- [ ] **Prepare a simple security overview** — One-pager on how the integration works, what data flows where, for their founder John

### Dempsey Team
- [ ] **Internal discussion** — Decide if they want to proceed with ChatGPT + Procore route
- [ ] **ChatGPT account cleanup** — Consolidate onto a team/enterprise plan
- [ ] **Procore admin access** — Provision for Justin when ready
- [ ] **IT conversation** — If server integration is desired, loop in IT team

---

## Pricing & Contract Notes

From pre-meeting research, the recommended range is **K-K fixed-price**. Given:
- This is their first AI integration
- Strong trust via Tom but still proving the value
- They already balked at Trunk Tools pricing
- The Procore connector will be reusable for future clients

**Recommendation: K fixed-price for Phase 1** (ChatGPT + Procore integration)
- Payment: 50% upfront / 50% on delivery, OR invoice on completion if you want to reduce friction
- Phase 2 (Monday.com integration, server access, agentic features) scoped separately

---

## Strategic Notes

### This client has high expansion potential
1. **Phase 1:** ChatGPT + Procore integration (K)
2. **Phase 2:** Monday.com integration — address the cross-department data silo problem
3. **Phase 3:** On-premise server access (requires IT partnership, higher trust)
4. **Phase 4:** Agentic capabilities — AI project engineer (their dream, your north star)

### Bryce is the decision-maker
- Drew and Merissa are the operational champions, but Bryce controls the purse and has the founder's ear
- Bryce's concerns: security, trust, long-term platform viability
- Address these directly in the proposal

### The "AI Project Engineer" vision is powerful
- Bryce named it explicitly — this is their aspirational end state
- Use this language in your proposal to show you understand where they want to go
- But be clear Phase 1 is retrieval/search, with a roadmap toward action/agentic
